product: team communication platform (similar to Slack or Microsoft Teams)

transcript.speaker: researcher
transcript.text: Tell me about your experience with your team's communication platform. How does it fit into your daily workflow?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: It's just something we use, I guess. Everyone's on it, so we have to be too. I mean, it's not like I have a choice or anything.

transcript.speaker: researcher
transcript.text: What does a typical day look like for you in terms of using the platform? Are there any specific features or tools that you rely on?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I don't know... I just get a ton of notifications all day. It's hard to keep up. I've tried to customize my settings, but it's just too much. Honestly, if something's really important, I'd rather just get an email about it.

transcript.speaker: researcher
transcript.text: Can you say more about that? What is it about email that you prefer for important things?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I think it's just because email feels more... permanent, I guess. Like, I know I can go back and find something later if I need to. With the platform, it's all just a bunch of noise. And if I miss something, it's hard to catch up.

transcript.speaker: researcher
transcript.text: That makes sense. Have you ever considered using a different communication platform, or is this one working well enough for your team?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I don't know... I mean, it's not like we love it or anything. It's just that switching would be such a hassle. We've all got our workflows set up, and it would be a big deal to change. So, we just kind of... stick with it, I guess.

transcript.speaker: researcher
transcript.text: I think I understand. So, it's more about the inertia of using something that's already established, rather than any particular affection for the platform itself. Is that a fair summary?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yeah, that's about right. I mean, it's not that I dislike it, I just... yeah. It's just what we use, you know?

transcript.speaker: researcher
transcript.text: One last question: looking ahead to the future, do you think your team's communication needs are going to change in any way?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I don't think so... I mean, we'll just probably just keep using this, I guess. It's not perfect, but it's good enough, and like I said, everyone's already on it. So, we'll just... make do, I suppose. And hey, at least it's consistent, right?
